Japanese: ‥‥そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong the trial.

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！
English: This case is extremely clear. I see no room for mis- interpretation of the facts.

Japanese: そ‥‥そんな‥‥ アタシじゃない！
English: I-It wasn't me! I swear it wasn't me!

Japanese: 毒入りのコーヒーを飲んだとき、 テーブルにいたのは被害者だけ‥‥
English: The evidence and testimony we have seen and heard are conclusive!

Japanese: ‥‥すべての証言・証拠が、 それを物語っています！
English: The victim was alone at his table when he drank from that poisoned cup of coffee.

Japanese: ちがう！　アタシ‥‥見たのに！ 本当に‥‥見たのに‥‥
English: No! You're wrong! I know what I saw...! I saw... I saw...!

Japanese: そうよ！　‥‥もうひとり、いた！ アイツが‥‥アイツが、犯人！
English: I saw someone else there! A man. He's the real killer!

Japanese: ‥‥どうして‥‥ だれも信じてくれないの！
English: Why won't anyone believe me?

Japanese: どうやら‥‥ 勝負あったようですな。
English: Well... I'd say that pretty much wraps this case up, wouldn't you...

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂くん！
English: ...Mr. Wright!?

Japanese: それでは‥‥被告人に、 判決を言い渡します。
English: This court finds the defendant...

Japanese: 本日は、これにて閉廷！
English: The court is adjourned.

Japanese: １月　６日　午前１０時　３分 成歩堂法律事務所
English: January 6, 10:03 AM Wright & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: いやあ。正月気分がぬけないねえ なるほどくん。
English: Ahh! The start of the new year always makes me feel like I can take on the whole world!

Japanese: そうだなあ。
English: I bet it does, Maya.

Japanese: そうだ。今年の成歩堂法律事務所の 抱負はズヴァリ！　“正月気分”。
English: So! I've decided that our resolution should be... Zvarri! "Take on the world!"

Japanese: ‥‥コレで行こうよ！
English: What do you think?

Japanese: どうでもいいけど、 食いすぎじゃないか？　おもち。
English: Sure, whatever, Maya... But I think maybe you've had more than enough mistletoe cake.

Japanese: いいのいいの。お正月といえば、 おもち食べながらみかん食べて、
English: Never! You've got to eat a lot of cake during New Year's! It's practically a tradition!

Japanese: おせちをつつきながらテレビ見て、 さらにトランプでババ抜きだから。
English: Like watching the fireworks on TV or playing a board game.

Japanese: コラアッ！
English: HEY, PAL!

Japanese: い、イトノコ刑事‥‥
English: Detective Gumshoe...

Japanese: あけまして おめでとうございます！
English: Happy New Year, Detective!

Japanese: う‥‥お、おめでとうございます。 コラ、アンタ！
English: Uh... likewise... NOW LISTEN UP, WRIGHT! I WANNA...

Japanese: 今年もよろしくおねがいします！
English: Here's to another fruitful year of lawyer-police cooperation!

Japanese: う‥‥よ、よろしく‥‥。 いいッスか！　アンタは‥‥
English: Um... Yeah... Me too... ALRIGHT, PAL! YOU'VE GOT SOME EXPLAINING TO...

Japanese: あ。イトノコさんイトノコさん。 お年玉ちょうだい！
English: Have you got a holiday present for me, Detective?

Japanese: う‥‥じゃ、じゃあこれ。 ケンソン抜きで少ないッスけど。
English: A what? Well... I, um... Here, have this. It-It's really nothing much, but...

Japanese: やったあ！
English: Yay! Thanks!

Japanese: 今日はハナシがあるッス！ そこに座るッス！
English: LOOK, PAL, WE NEED TO HAVE A TALK. TAKE A SEAT!

Japanese: はみちゃんのぶんも。
English: Hey! What about Pearly? You haven't forgotten her present, have you?

Japanese: う‥‥は、はい‥‥って、 ワザとやってないッス？
English: Ah... N-No... I mean, yes... I mean, no... Are you doing this on purpose?

Japanese: バレちゃいました？ 初わらい、ってヤツですね！
English: Ha ha, guess I'm busted. How did you like my first practical joke of the year?

Japanese: ハッ！　コレを見ても 笑っていられるッスかッ！
English: Very funny, pal. Now let's see how funny you think it is when I show you this!

Japanese: ‥‥雑誌‥‥ですか？
English: What is it? A magazine?

Japanese: えー、なになに。
English: Hey, I wanna see!

Japanese: 《毒殺事件に有罪判決。 成歩堂弁護士、惨敗》
English: "Deadly Poisoning Brings Guilty Verdict. Defense Attorney Wright Trounced."

Japanese: ざ、ザンパイ‥‥？ ちょ、ちょっと貸して！
English: Tr-Trounced!? Let me see that!

Japanese: “昨日の裁判での弁護士は、 まさにシロウト以下で‥‥”
English: "The defense attorney gave an almost childishly amateur performance yesterday..."

Japanese: ‥‥なんだコレ？
English: What the heck is this!?

Japanese: アンタのコトッス！
English: It's a report, pal. About you!

Japanese: “有罪判決のセキニンのすべては、 成歩堂弁護士にある”だって。
English: Listen to this! "Mr. Wright must take full responsibility for the ruling in this case."

Japanese: どッス！　おぼえがないとは 言わせねッス！
English: WELL!? And don't tell me you don't remember anything about it!

Japanese: お、おぼえがありませんよ！
English: But I don't remember anything about it!

Japanese: だいたい、いつの雑誌ですか？ それ。
English: When was that issue from anyway?

Japanese: えーと。‥‥去年の１２月ッスね。
English: Umm... December of last year, which I guess makes it last month.

Japanese: 古雑誌じゃないですか。
English: Which makes it old news, you mean.

Japanese: 去年の１２月に、 毒殺事件なんて扱ってませんよ！
English: But I wasn't involved in a poisoning case in December!

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことなのかな？
English: Hmm, so what do you think this is all about, Nick?

Japanese: これがアンタじゃないとすると ‥‥考えられるのは、１つッス。
English: If it wasn't you, pal, then that leaves only one possibility...

Japanese: ま。まさか‥‥‥
English: No way. You don't mean...
A pho...

Japanese: ニセなるほどくん。
English: A phony Nick!?

Japanese: （‥‥初わらいだな、たしかに）
English: (This must be Gumshoe's idea of a joke. Guess he's starting off the year with one, too...)

Japanese: 《雑誌の記事》を 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Magazine Clipping added to the Court Record.

Japanese: さー、どうしてくれるッス！
English: SO!? What are you gonna do about it, pal!?

Japanese: どうする、って言われましても‥‥
English: What do you mean, what am I going to do about it?

Japanese: でも。でも。なるほどくんのせいで 有罪になっちゃったんだよ！
English: Well, it's your fault that the judge found the defendant guilty in this case!

Japanese: ぼ、ぼくのせい‥‥？
English: My fault!? How do you figure that?

Japanese: なるほどくん、最近ちょっと 有名になってきたからねー。
English: Because THE Phoenix Wright is super famous now! Well, maybe only sort of...

Japanese: チョーシに乗るから、 こういうコトになるッス！
English: Yeah. See what happens when you hotshots start getting too full of yourselves?

Japanese: （ううう‥‥ ぼくがワルいのか‥‥）
English: (But I didn't do anything wrong! ...At least, not that I can remember...)

Japanese: キチンとオワビに行くッス！ 今からッス！　すぐにッス！
English: You better make this right, pal. Now!

Japanese: そして、裁判をやりなおすッス！
English: And that means taking the case back to court. Got it?

Japanese: “正月気分”で引き受ける、 今年最初の事件になりそうだね！
English: Sounds like we've got our first case of the new year! Let's tackle it with gusto!

Japanese: でも‥‥この事件、一度は 有罪判決を受けてるんだぞ。
English: I don't know... The judge already issued a guilty verdict once in this case.

Japanese: カンタンにひっくり返せるワケ、 ないじゃないか。
English: It's not going to be easy to get it overturned.

Japanese: ザンネンだけど、“正月気分”は 来年の抱負にしようか。
English: I guess that New Year's resolution is going to have to wait until next year.

Japanese: じゃッ！　自分はこれから、 裁判所のほうに行くッスから！
English: So you're taking the case, right!? Good! I'm gonna head over to the courthouse then.

Japanese: なんかあったら、ケーサツの 自分のところへ来るッス！
English: After that, I'll go back to the precinct. Drop by if you need something, OK pal!?

Japanese: （たしかに“成歩堂　龍一”の名前 も、少しは知られてきたようだ）
English: (I guess people are starting to know the name, "Phoenix Wright".)

Japanese: （依頼人が、その名前を信頼して ぼくに弁護を頼んだのなら‥‥）
English: (If a client entrusted a case to me based on my reputation...)

Japanese: （ぼくにも、セキニンが あるのかもしれない）
English: (I guess I am kind of responsible.)

Japanese: （‥‥それにしても‥‥ ぼくの“ニセモノ”なんて‥‥）
English: (But why would someone want to impersonate me?)

Japanese: （いったい、どんなヤツなんだ？）
English: (What sort of a guy would do that?)

Japanese: 観葉植物のチャーリーくん。
English: Charley. A quite decorative plant.

Japanese: 今は亡くなってしまった所長、 千尋さんの形見‥‥だ。
English: He's sort of a keepsake... Something to remember Mia by.

Japanese: 散らかりほうだいの事務所だけど、 こいつの世話だけは忘れない。
English: Sure, the office is a mess, but I never forget to water this little fella.

Japanese: ムズカシイ法律の本が あざ笑うように並んでいる。
English: Difficult-looking legal books stand in a formidable row. They mock me.

Japanese: ‥‥そういえば、うっすらと ホコリをかぶってるな。
English: ...Actually, I've neglected them for so long that they're covered in a layer of dust.

Japanese: たまには‥‥せめて 読むフリだけでもしてみるか。
English: I guess I should at least pretend to read them once in a while.

Japanese: ぼくのデスクだ。
English: It's my desk.

Japanese: あまり座る機会がないため、 いやにキチンとかたづいている。
English: I don't get to use it much, so it's still super neat and tidy from when Pearls cleaned it.

Japanese: 窓の向かいがわには、 巨大なビルがそびえている。
English: There is a giant building just outside the window.

Japanese: 《ホテル・バンドー》 超ゴウカな超一流ホテルだ。
English: It's the Gatewater Hotel, a high-class, luxury hotel.

Japanese: なんでも、となり街の一角を ゴッソリ買収して、
English: The chain is getting so rich that they bought a whole chunk of the next town over...

Japanese: 超レジャー施設《バンドーランド》 の建設が始まったらしい。
English: ...and started building a huge theme park. It's going to be called "Gatewater Land".

Japanese: ‥‥そういえば、あのボーイさん から年賀状が来てたっけ。
English: ...I wonder if that bellboy is going to send me a greeting card this year, too?

Japanese: 古い映画のポスターだ。
English: An old movie poster.

Japanese: ムカシ千尋さんが見て、 初めて泣いた映画なのだそうだ。
English: Apparently, this was the first movie that made Mia cry when she saw it, a long time ago.

Japanese: 最近、妹の真宵ちゃんが見て、 一晩中泣いていたらしい。
English: Maya watched it recently, and said she cried all night, too!

Japanese: ‥‥ということで、最近は このポスターを張ってある。
English: ...Which, I guess, is why it's back up on the wall.

Japanese: 今度、ぼくも見てみるか。
English: I'll have to check it out one of these days.

Japanese: さて。どうしようか、これから。
English: So, what's our first move?

Japanese: どうする、って。 留置所に行って、話を聞いたり‥‥
English: I guess we go down to the detention center and talk--

Japanese: でもさ。相手は、なるほどくんの せいで有罪になったヒトだよ。
English: Wait a sec, Nick. This person's behind bars because of you.

Japanese: どんなカオして会えばいいの？
English: Whoever it is isn't going to be jumping at the chance to meet you, right?

Japanese: いやいや！　でも、それは ニセモノだったワケだろ？
English: Hey, hey! Let's get one thing straight. It wasn't me, it was a fake me that did this!

Japanese: やっぱり、カオもソックリなのかな ‥‥なるほどくんのニセモノ。
English: Hmm, I wonder if he looks exactly like you... Your phony, Xin Eohp, I mean.

Japanese: （ブキミな話だな‥‥）
English: (I hope not. *shudder* ...And what kind of name for an evil double is Xin Eohp anyway!?)

Japanese: あ。もしかして、なるほどくん！
English: Ah! Nick! I've got it!

Japanese: 双子の兄弟なら、いないぞ。
English: If you're going to ask whether I've got a twin brother, the answer is no.

Japanese: ‥‥つまんないの。
English: ...Spoilsport.

Japanese: 今日のイトノコさん、 なんかヘンだったよねー。
English: Did you notice Gumshoe was acting weirder than usual, or was it just me?

Japanese: ‥‥なにが？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: ものスゴく あたふたしてたじゃない。
English: I mean, he was really worked up.

Japanese: 初めてムスコさんが生まれた パパさながらに。
English: Like a guy who's just found out he's going to be a dad or something.

Japanese: そういえば‥‥たしかに。
English: Yeah, I guess he was acting kind of strange.

Japanese: 強い思い入れがあるのかな。 なるほどくんに。
English: Maybe he realized he's got strong feelings for you, Nick!

Japanese: ‥‥きもちわるいコト 言うなよ。
English: Considering how we interact, I seriously doubt that, Maya...

Japanese: なるほどくんのせいで コーフンしてたんじゃなければ‥‥
English: Well, if he wasn't nervous because of you...

Japanese: その原因は、 有罪になった依頼人‥‥かな。
English: ...then maybe it's because of our new guilty client?

Japanese: まだ持ってたんだ、そのバッジ。
English: So you've still got that badge, I see.

Japanese: そ、そりゃそうだろう。 弁護士なんだから。
English: Huh!? Well, I'm a lawyer, aren't I?

Japanese: いやー、こんなに長く つづくとは思わなかったよ。
English: Yeah, but I guess I just didn't think you'd keep on being one for this long.

Japanese: あぶなっかしいからねー、 なるほどくんのベンゴ。
English: You have to admit, you've had some close brushes with death because of your job.

Japanese: （それは、真宵ちゃんの霊媒も 似たようなモノだと思うけど）
English: (...I fail to see how being a lawyer is more dangerous than channeling dead people.)

Japanese: どうかな。これなんだけど。
English: Do you know anything about this, Maya?

Japanese: うーん‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: さすがのあたしも、この世のすべて を知ってるワケじゃないからねー。
English: Even someone as worldly-wise as me doesn't know everything about everything, Nick.

Japanese: それについては、なんとも 言えないな。さすがのあたしも。
English: So I, the great Maya, am sad to say I can't tell you much about this object.

Japanese: （そこまでキタイしてない）
English: (A simple yes or no, Maya... It's not as if you had to give me a dissertation...)

Japanese: それにしても、 なるほどくんのニセモノかあ。
English: I can't believe you've got an impersonator.

Japanese: メーワクな話だよな。
English: I can't either. *sigh*

Japanese: あ。でも、モノマネされる 芸能人は一流だっていうじゃない！
English: Aww, but look on the bright side. Only famous stars have people impersonating them!

Japanese: “モノマネ”じゃなくて “ニセモノ”だしなあ。
English: This isn't funny, Maya. It's not just an impersonation. This guy stole my identity!

Japanese: ヒトが有罪になってるんだ。 ‥‥シャレにならないよ。
English: And someone may have been wrongly convicted as a result.

Japanese: 芸能人になればよかったのにね、 なるほどくん。
English: ...Guess you should've gone into showbiz, huh, Nick?

Japanese: （‥‥それは マッタク関係ないと思う）
English: (...*sigh* I will never understand how your mind works, Maya.)

Japanese: 《クリーニング・ボンバー》 ‥‥ねえ。
English: Hmm... "MC Bomber"...

Japanese: それに、《イッセンマン》だぞ。 "100,000", too.
English: And don't forget the

Japanese: なんか、どこか引っかかるね。 《クリーニング・ボンバー》。
English: There's something about that name, "MC Bomber".

Japanese: たしかに‥‥ 気になる名前だよなあ。
English: Yeah. It's a pretty unusual name.

Japanese: 今日び《ボンバー》だもんねえ。
English: Everyone's worried about bombs these days, I guess.

Japanese: （‥‥そういうイミじゃ ないんだけど‥‥）
English: (I'm not sure that has any bearing on anything related to this case...)

Japanese: とにかく、ちゃんと調べて みたほうがいいね！
English: Anyway, we should really look into this some more!

Japanese: どうなの？　霊媒師の 修行のほうは。
English: So? How's your spirit medium training going?

Japanese: ‥‥うん。ちゃんと 打たれてるよ。シャワーに。
English: Well... I stand under the rushing waters of the shower head every day.

Japanese: それは“浴びてる”って 言うんだよ。滝のほうは？
English: ...That's called "taking a shower". What happened to the rushing waters of a waterfall?

Japanese: ちょっと、お休み。 なんかね。ダメなんだ、今。
English: Well, I'm kind of taking a break... I'm having a bit of trouble right now, you know?

Japanese: （１年前の、あの事件から‥‥）
English: (Last year's incident must still be on her mind...)

Japanese: （修行している真宵ちゃんを 見なくなった）
English: (I haven't seen Maya train at all since that time.)

Japanese: （“迷っている”‥‥千尋さんが そう言ってたっけ‥‥）
English: (I think Mia said that it's because Maya's at a loss these days...)

Japanese: めずらしいよね、 イトノコさん。
English: Digging up an old case that's already been ruled on...

Japanese: 有罪が決まった事件を ほじくり返すなんて。
English: That's not like Gumshoe, is it?

Japanese: たしかに。いつも目の前の事件 だけでアップアップしてるのに。
English: No. He barely manages to stay on top of the cases in front of him.

Japanese: 忙しいわ給料は安いわで、 コートもかえないし。
English: Yeah, he's always busy, and gets dirt pay. I mean, look at that dusty old coat he wears!

Japanese: （“替えない”なのか “買えない”なのか‥‥？）
English: (Did you mean he "gets paid dirt" or do you really think they pay him in gravel?)

Japanese: ‥‥こないだの裁判で、 また助けてもらっちゃったな。
English: Mia helped me out in court again last time.

Japanese: いいね、なるほどくんは お姉ちゃんに会えて。
English: It's so cool that you get to meet my sis like that.
Yeah...

Japanese: あたしたち‥‥霊媒中は、 イシキがとぎれちゃうから。
English: We mediums lose consciousness when we're channeling, so I don't get to see my sis...

Japanese: ‥‥そういえば、 そうだったね。
English: ...I never really thought about it that way.

Japanese: あ！　でも‥‥ 千尋さんに会いたかったら、
English: Hey! If you want to meet Mia...

Japanese: 春美ちゃんに呼び出して もらえばいいじゃないか。
English: ...why don't we just have Pearls call her?

Japanese: ううん‥‥ それは、できないよ。
English: No... I couldn't do that.

Japanese: はみちゃんに、シンパイ かけちゃうからね。
English: I'd be too worried about her.

Japanese: （‥‥真宵ちゃんも、 いろいろ考えてるんだな）
English: (I guess Maya's got all sorts of things to think about, too.)

Japanese: （‥‥見かけによらず）
English: (...Despite her immaturity saying otherwise.)

Japanese: ケッキョク、まだ思い出せないの？ ゴドー検事のコト。
English: So you still can't remember anything about Godot, huh?

Japanese: い、いやいや。だから、 ぼくのほうは知らないんだよ！
English: I already told you. I have no idea who he is, let alone why he dislikes me so much.

Japanese: なんだか、なるほどくんに 恨みでもあるみたいだし。
English: Well he sure seems to have a problem with you for some reason.

Japanese: 気をつけてね。 コーヒー好きはハラ黒いから。
English: You'd better watch your back, Nick. Coffee lovers can be quite bitter.

Japanese: 勝手なコト言うなよ。
English: You're just making that up, Maya!

Japanese: マコちゃん‥‥２回目だね。 殺人容疑でつかまるの。
English: This is Maggey's second time as a murder suspect, huh?

Japanese: もう、１年以上たつのか。 この前の事件から‥‥
English: Yeah. The first time was a little over a year ago...

Japanese: あのころは、婦人警官の 制服を着ていたっけ。
English: She was on the police force back then.

Japanese: イトノコさんのブカだって 言ってたよね、たしか。
English: Wasn't she working under Gumshoe?

Japanese: ‥‥今度も、 ゼッタイ助けてあげようね！
English: That does it, Nick! We're gonna get her off the hook or I'll give up hamburgers!

Japanese: どうかな。このヒトなんだけど。
English: Do you know anything about this person?

Japanese: うーん‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: さすがのあたしも、世界中のみんな とトモダチ、ってワケじゃないし。
English: Even someone as worldly-wise as me doesn't know everything about everyone, Nick.

Japanese: そのヒトについては、なんとも 言えないな。さすがのあたしも。
English: So I, the great Maya, am sad to say I can't tell you much about this individual.

Japanese: （そこまでキタイしてない）
English: (A simple yes or no, Maya... It's not as if you had to give me a dissertation...)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 成歩堂法律事務所
English: January 6 Wright & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: やれやれ‥‥本土坊さん、 真宵ちゃんを気に入ったみたいだ。
English: Poor Maya. It looks like Mr. Armstrong's really taken a shine to her.

Japanese: しかたない。彼女には しばらく、はたらいてもらうか。
English: I suppose I'll just have to let her work at the restaurant for a while.

Japanese: ‥‥ほとぼりがさめたころ、 吐麗美庵にむかえに行こう。
English: I'll go pick her up from Trés Bien once things have cooled off.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: January 6 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: うう。ドキドキするねー。 どんなヒトだったんだろ？
English: This is so nerve-wracking, waiting to meet our new client.

Japanese: なるほどくんにダマされて 有罪にされちゃったの。
English: I wonder just what kind of person you tricked and got found guilty...?

Japanese: ひ、ヒト聞きの悪いこと 言わないでほしいな。
English: K-Keep it down, Maya! That kind of talk could ruin me!

Japanese: ああああああッ！
English: Aaaaaaaah!

Japanese: 成歩堂さん、ヒドいです！ このアタシを見捨てるなんて！
English: How could you, Mr. Wright!? How could you do this to me!?

Japanese: 今も独房でひとり、 すすり泣いていたッス！
English: They put me in solitary! I haven't been able to stop crying.

Japanese: き、きみは‥‥たしか‥‥
English: A-Aren't... you...

Japanese: もースズキ、 人間不信になりそうッス！
English: Yes, I am! I am totally and utterly let down!

Japanese: あっ！　も、もしかして、 あなた‥‥
English: Aah! You're... Are you...?

Japanese: “もしかして”じゃないッスよ！ マコです。須々木　マコ！
English: Don't pretend you don't know me! It's me, Maggey, remember!? Maggey Byrde!

Japanese: 須々木　マコって‥‥ああ！
English: Maggey Byrde... Ah!

Japanese: （須々木　マコ‥‥。以前、 弁護した婦人警官だ‥‥）
English: (Maggey Byrde... She's the policewoman I defended that one time.)

Japanese: （たしか、コイビトの警察官を 殺害した容疑だった）
English: (She was accused of murdering her lover. He was a cop, too.)

Japanese: ど、どうしてこんなところに！ 無罪になったはずじゃあ‥‥
English: What are you doing in here!? Didn't I get you acquitt--

Japanese: うわあ！　よくそんな コトが言えますね！
English: Oh, sure! Very funny!

Japanese: あれだけザツな弁護で ヒトに有罪、カッ食らわしといて！
English: After that fifth-rate defense job, you come in here and start making jokes!?

Japanese: ダメだよなるほどくん。 ちゃんと事情を説明しないと！
English: You better hurry up and tell her what happened, Nick.

Japanese: そ、そうだったんですか‥‥
English: O-Oh... I see...

Japanese: そ、そうだったのか‥‥
English: So that's where we stand right now.

Japanese: ‥‥また、事件に 巻きこまれちゃったんだ‥‥
English: I'm sorry you've been caught up in another murder...

Japanese: スズキの人生は、まさに不運と 敗北と大番狂わせの見本市ッス。
English: My whole life has been nothing but a whirlwind of bad luck and failures.

Japanese: （この前も、たしか そんなコト言ってたな‥‥）
English: (I vaguely remember her saying the exact same thing last time...)

Japanese: ヘーキッス！　また１つ、デッカイ 不幸をしょいこんだだけッス。
English: But I don't mind! What's one more disaster in my life?

Japanese: こうして、ホンモノの成歩堂さんも 来てくれたことだし‥‥
English: At least now the real Mr. Wright is here with me.

Japanese: スズキ、ヘコたれません！
English: I won't let the world keep me down, sir!

Japanese: 成歩堂さん‥‥。もう一度、 できるんですか？　‥‥裁判。
English: Mr. Wright... Do you think it's possible to get a retrial?

Japanese: たぶんね。弁護士がいないまま 判決が下されたんだから。
English: Probably. The court ruled in the absence of a genuine defense attorney.

Japanese: 再審理はできると思うよ。
English: So we should be able to get a retrial.

Japanese: あの‥‥成歩堂さん。
English: Um... Mr. Wright...?

Japanese: 今度は‥‥勝てますか？
English: Do you think we'll win next time? ...Sir?

Japanese: たしかにスズキ、不幸のフルコース に敗北のデザートつきの人生ッス。
English: My life has been a full course meal of bad luck, complete with defeat for dessert.

Japanese: 生後６ヶ月のとき、マンションの ９階から落っこちたのを皮切りに、
English: Since I was 6 months old, when I fell from the 9th floor of my apartment building...

Japanese: ひととおりの乗り物にはひかれ、 ひととおりの食べ物にはあたり、
English: ...I've been hit by all sorts of vehicles, gotten sick from all sorts of foods...

Japanese: だいたいの試験には落第して、 ほとんどの災害をも経験して、
English: ...failed at almost every test I've taken, experienced almost every kind of disaster...

Japanese: 殺人の罪を着せられれば、弁護士の ニセモノをつかまされる始末。
English: I even landed a phony lawyer when I had the misfortune of being accused of murder.

Japanese: ‥‥でも！
English: But!

Japanese: それでも、このスズキは 生きているッス！　今も！
English: I will survive! Because Maggey Byrde always lives to fight another day!

Japanese: だから！　いつか 見つけられるハズッス！
English: And one day, I'll find it! Just you wait and see, sir!

Japanese: ‥‥ヒトなみのシアワセをッ！
English: I'll find that one, single moment of good luck!

Japanese: くっそー、 ニセなるほどくんめぇ‥‥
English: Argh, Xin Eohp is really gonna pay for this!

Japanese: な、なんでぼくを ニラむんだよ‥‥
English: Wh-What are you staring at me like that for...?

Japanese: （とはいえ‥‥）
English: (But Maya's right...)

Japanese: （ぼくの名前を使って、 殺人の罪を着せるなんて‥‥）
English: (Whoever it is that thought it was a good idea to use my name...)

Japanese: （‥‥ユルせるわけがない！）
English: (...and get an innocent girl convicted of murder had better watch out!)

Japanese: ゼッタイに見つけだそうね！ なるほどくんのニセモノ‥‥
English: We'll find him! Don't you worry. We'll get Xin Eohp for you!

Japanese: じゃあ、吐麗美庵の場所を 教えますねッ！
English: Thank you! Oh! I'll tell you where Trés Bien is then!

Japanese: とれび‥‥ああ、事件が 起こったレストランか。
English: Trés... Ah, right. The restaurant where the murder took place.

Japanese: はい！　店長によろしく 言っといてください！
English: Yes, sir. When you go, please tell Mr. Armstrong I said hi!

Japanese: よし！　なるほどくん。 さっそく行ってみようか！
English: Sure! Alright, Nick! Let's go check out this restaurant and its food!

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitors the visitor's room.

Japanese: ウエイトレスさんがめずらしい のだろうか。
English: I bet they don't get too many waitresses in here.

Japanese: ちょっと そわそわしている。
English: He looks like he's getting a bit restless.

Japanese: ごていねいに、カメラで 見張っているようだ。
English: Smile, you're on candid camera.

Japanese: マコちゃん。どうしたの？ そのカッコウ‥‥
English: So, how come you're dressed like that, Maggey?

Japanese: 去年は、婦人警官の 制服でパリッとキメてたのに。
English: Last year you looked so sharp in that police uniform.

Japanese: あの事件のおかげで、スズキ、 ケーサツをクビになっちゃって。
English: Hmph. I was fired after that... incident last year.

Japanese: そ、そうなんだ‥‥
English: Oh. I'm sorry.

Japanese: ゼーンゼン、かまいません！
English: It's OK. I don't mind one bit.

Japanese: ケーサツのフンイキはまあ、 じゅうぶん楽しみましたから。
English: I enjoyed being on the force, but I think it was time for me to move on.

Japanese: それで‥‥今は？
English: So, what do you do now?

Japanese: スズキの人生の第２章は、 ウエイトレスさんだったッス！
English: In the second act of "The Life of Maggey Byrde", I'm playing the role of a waitress!

Japanese: うえいとれす‥‥
English: A waitress!?

Japanese: フランス料理のレストランです！ ちっちゃいけど、カワイイお店で。
English: Yes. In a French restaurant. It's a small place, but it's quite fashionable.

Japanese: この愛くるしいスマイルと、なやま しいボディラインのおかげで、
English: My charming smile and shapely figure came through for me.

Japanese: サッソク、採用されたッス！
English: And the owner, Mr. Armstrong, hired me straight away, sir!

Japanese: ‥‥で、サッソク事件に 巻きこまれたわけだ。
English: And then you got into this mess straight away, right?

Japanese: う。まあ‥‥。
English: Yeah, you could put it that way...

Japanese: あのオソロシイ事件は、 先月の３日‥‥
English: This whole mess started on the 3rd of last month...

Japanese: 吐麗美庵（とれびあん）で 発生したッス！
English: And it happened at Trés Bien.

Japanese: とれびあん‥‥？
English: "Treh Bee-an"...?

Japanese: このスズキが、エガオとメシを 運んでいたレストランです！
English: Yes. It's a restaurant where good service and a friendly smile are always included!

Japanese: はあ‥‥
English: Oh...

Japanese: テーブルで、２人の男が コーヒーを飲んでいたッス。
English: There were two men at the table, both drinking coffee.

Japanese: そして‥‥
English: And then...

Japanese: 男は、相手のコーヒーに 毒を入れたんです！
English: One of the men slipped some poison into the victim's cup!

Japanese: 被害者は、コーヒーをヒトクチ飲む なり『ぎゃあ』ってなモンでした！
English: The victim took just one sip and was gasping for air!

Japanese: アタシ‥‥あまりのコトに、キゼツ して、ぶっ倒れちゃったッス。
English: I was so shocked, I passed out.

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっと待って、マコちゃん。
English: Hey, hold on there, Maggey.

Japanese: なんですか？
English: What?

Japanese: さっきから“被害者”“被害者” って言ってるけど‥‥
English: You keep calling the guy, "the victim".

Japanese: 知ってるヒトじゃないの？ コロされちゃったヒト。
English: Didn't you know the guy who was killed?

Japanese: し、知らないッス！ 初めて見るカオだったッス！
English: Not at all! I'd never even seen the guy before!

Japanese: そうなんだ‥‥
English: Oh...

Japanese: （じゃあ、殺害する 動機もないワケだよな‥‥）
English: (So she wouldn't have had a motive to kill him then, I guess...)

Japanese: 犯人はその、もう１人の男だ。 ‥‥見たんだよね？　現場を。
English: And the other man, the killer. You saw him, right?

Japanese: そのとおりッス！ ウエイトレスは見ていたッス！
English: Of course! A good waitress must be attentive to the clientele.

Japanese: ‥‥なのにどうして、 きみが有罪になってるの？
English: So, you saw the killer, but you were found guilty of the crime anyway? How come?

Japanese: 成歩堂さんに言われたくないッス！
English: You tell me, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ううう‥‥ （ぼくの“ニセモノ”か‥‥）
English: Ack! (Guess the answer to my question is "my phony"...)

Japanese: （彼女は、そいつを見ている。 ‥‥できれば、情報がほしいな）
English: (Anyway, she saw the killer. Better see if I can get a description of the guy.)

Japanese: ハンニンを目撃したのに‥‥ どうして、有罪に？
English: So, if you saw the murderer, why were you still convicted?

Japanese: それが‥‥ ダレも見ていないッス。
English: Because no one else saw.

Japanese: 見ていない‥‥？
English: Saw what?

Japanese: 毒を入れた、もう１人の男。
English: The other man. The one who put the poison in the victim's coffee.

Japanese: ‥‥店長も、レストランのお客も、 みーんな、こう証言したッス。
English: Everyone testified that way. Mr. Armstrong, the customer... Everyone.

Japanese: 『被害者のテーブルには、 ずっと１人しかいなかった』‥‥
English: "The victim was sitting alone at his table the whole time."

Japanese: な‥‥なんだって！
English: But how's that possible?

Japanese: ダレも‥‥。ダレひとり、スズキの 言うことを信じてくれなくて。
English: I don't know. But nobody, not one person, would believe me, sir.

Japanese: 最後の望みをたくした成歩堂さん にも、キレイにウラ切られて。
English: Even Phoenix Wright, my one last hope for a fair trial, failed me.

Japanese: あの弁護はないッス！　うちの おばあちゃんのほうがマシッス。
English: What a pathetic defense! My granny could've done a better job!

Japanese: い、いや！　だからそれは、 ぼくじゃなかったんだから‥‥
English: Look! That wasn't me, OK?

Japanese: ‥‥それに。
English: And then!

Japanese: スズキのエプロンのポッケから、 トンでもないモノが出てきたッス。
English: ...They found something a bit incriminating in my apron pocket.

Japanese: な‥‥なに？
English: What?

Japanese: 毒薬の小ビン‥‥ッス。
English: A small bottle... of poison.

Japanese: ええええええええええッ！ ど、毒が‥‥ポケットから？
English: WHAT!? P-Poison!? It was in your pocket!?

Japanese: ‥‥被害者が倒れたとき、 スズキもキゼツしちゃったッス。
English: Well, I passed out when the victim collapsed.

Japanese: きっと‥‥そのとき、ハンニンが ポッケに‥‥
English: The killer must've slipped the poison into my pocket when I was unconscious.

Japanese: だれも、見ていないのか‥‥ その“もう１人の男”を。
English: And no one else saw this other guy?

Japanese: そッス。『見てない』って。 そんなハズ、ないのに‥‥。
English: No, sir. That's what everyone said. But I don't see how they could've missed him...

Japanese: モンダイのあの２人にコーヒーを 運んだの、アタシだったッス。
English: I was the one who took the coffee to the two men.

Japanese: お、そうなんだ。‥‥その人たち、 どんな感じだった？
English: O-Oh...? And what was your impression of them?

Japanese: うーん。最初２人を見たときは、 音楽カンケイの人たちかな、って。
English: Well... When I first saw them, I kind of thought they might be in the music industry.

Japanese: おんがく‥‥？
English: In music? How come?

Japanese: １人はイヤホンをつけて ムズカシイ顔をしていたし、
English: Well, one of them had some sort of earpiece, and an emo musician's look about him.

Japanese: テーブルの上には、見本のＣＤが 置いてあったッス。
English: And there was a sample CD on the table, sir.

Japanese: イヤホンに‥‥見本のＣＤ、かあ。 どんなＣＤだったんですか？
English: An earpiece and a sample CD, huh? Did you get a look at the CD at all?

Japanese: サインペンで、ウラ面に バンドの名前が書いてあったッス。
English: It had a band's name written on it.

Japanese: 《クリ》なんとか‥‥って。 きっと、デビュー前ッスね。
English: I think it was "MC"-something. They must've been preparing for their debut, I guess.

Japanese: ふうん‥‥バンドねえ。 売りこみ、だったのかな。
English: So it was a band's CD. Maybe a promo disc?

Japanese: 《くりきんとん》かあ‥‥。
English: Maybe it was "MC Screwdriver"!

Japanese: 勝手に名前つけるなよ。
English: Get serious, Maya. Would you buy the CD of a group named that?

Japanese: あのバンド名、なんだっけな。 ‥‥“クリ”‥‥
English: Nngh... What was the name of that group again...? "MC Hacksaw..."? No... "MC..."

Japanese: で？　で？　ケッキョク、どんな ヤツだったんですか！　ハンニン。
English: And what about the killer? What did he look like?

Japanese: そ‥‥それが。 よくおぼえてないッス。
English: Well, I, um... I don't really remember.

Japanese: 被害者と同じく、体格のいい おニイさんだった、ぐらいしか‥‥
English: Only that he was a young man. Well-built like the victim, really.

Japanese: あ、そのバッジ！ ‥‥ホンモノッスか？
English: That badge! Is it real?

Japanese: ほ、ホンモノだよ！
English: Of course it's real!

Japanese: 一度ダマされたニンゲンは、 うたぐり深くなるッス。
English: That's what they all say. But I've been duped before.

Japanese: 貸してくださいッ！
English: Give it to me for a sec!

Japanese: ‥‥かりっ！
English: ...*chomp!*

Japanese: バッジを噛んでるよ。
English: She... She bit into it...

Japanese: ちょっぴり歯形がついたッス。
English: And I left a few teeth marks, too!

Japanese: はあ。
English: I can see that...

Japanese: ‥‥それが何をイミするのか わからないのがツラいところッス。
English: I just wish I could remember if that means it's real or a fake.

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。 （ホンモノなのに）
English: *sigh* (I said it was real!)

Japanese: そうだ！　コイツのことを 聞いとかないと！
English: Oh, yeah. I need to ask you about this...

Japanese: あ！　スズキ惨敗の記事ッスね！
English: Hey! This article's about my case!

Japanese: この、ぼくのニセモノの 話を聞かせてくれないかな。
English: Can you tell me anything about the guy who was pretending to be me?

Japanese: リョーカイッス！
English: Yes, sir!

Japanese: あれは、アタシがタイホされた 翌朝のことでした。
English: It was the morning after I'd been arrested...

Japanese: いつものスーツでビシッとキメて、 面会室に来てくれたッス。
English: I met you in the visitor's room here. You were wearing one of your super-sharp suits.

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくが？
English: Me?

Japanese: ええ。成歩堂さんが。
English: Yes, you, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ううう‥‥
English: Argh...

Japanese: ね。ね。あたしのニセモノも いました？
English: Hey, Maggey! Was my evil double Ayam here, too?

Japanese: そういえば、ニセ真宵さんは いなかったなあ‥‥。
English: No. I don't remember a phony you, Maya.

Japanese: ‥‥ちぇ。 ちょっと、ザンネン。
English: Oh... *sigh* It would've been so cool...

Japanese: アットー的なハクリョクで このスズキを、かきクドいたッス。
English: Then you got really worked up and passionate.

Japanese: 『かならずアンタを ムザイにしたるわ！』‥‥って。
English: "I'm gonna get you cleared of this crime!" you said.

Japanese: それはいいけど‥‥そもそも、 ぼくと初対面じゃないのに‥‥
English: OK, I get the picture. But you've met me in person before...

Japanese: どうして、ニセモノだって 気がつかなかったの？
English: So how come you didn't realize that guy wasn't the real me?

Japanese: たしかに、今、思うと ちょっとヘンだったッス。
English: I guess, looking back now, it was a little strange...

Japanese: ちょっと‥‥？
English: Only a little?

Japanese: いつもよりちょっと背が高かったし ちょっと目つきがワルかったし、
English: Well, OK, so you were a bit taller than normal. And you looked a bit shady.

Japanese: ちょっと声も割れてたし、 ちょっと関西弁でしゃべってたし、
English: And your voice was a bit weird. Oh, and you had this kind of funny accent. And...

Japanese: ‥‥ゼンゼンちがうじゃないか！
English: So the guy was nothing like me, then!?

Japanese: でも。アタマはギザギザだったし、 ブルーのスーツだったし。
English: But he had your spiky hair and blue suit.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: Is that all it takes for someone to imitate me...?)

Japanese: 法廷のヒトたちも‥‥ 気がつかなかったの？
English: How about everyone else in the courtroom, like the judge and the observers...?

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長さんとか。
English: Didn't they realize he was an imposter?

Japanese: みんな、カオに“？”マークが うかんでましたけど。
English: Everyone had these big question marks on their faces...

Japanese: なーんか、言い出せない フンイキだったッス。
English: But it seemed that no one wanted to say anything, sir.

Japanese: ビミョーな話だなあ‥‥
English: This case just keeps getting weirder and weirder...

Japanese: おッ！　スポーツ新聞ッスか！
English: Oh! A sports paper.

Japanese: どれどれ。タソガレ山は 全勝を守ったかな‥‥
English: Let's see, let's see... Did Gutsen Brawn manage to defend his heavyweight title?

Japanese: 悪いけど、この新聞‥‥１ヶ月前、 事件当日のものなんだ。
English: Sorry, Maggey. That paper is actually a month old... It's from the day of the murder...

Japanese: ちなみに、タソガレ山、 きのうハリ倒されてましたよ。
English: And Gutsen got knocked out yesterday, I'm afraid.

Japanese: うううう‥‥
English: Oh, no...

Japanese: これ、吐麗美庵のマガジンラックで 見つけたんだけど。
English: I found this paper in the magazine rack at Trés Bien.

Japanese: え‥‥ それは、オカシイッスね。
English: Really? That's strange...

Japanese: ウチのお店、新聞は 置いてないはずッスけど。
English: Trés Bien doesn't get newspapers.

Japanese: ‥‥店長が、キライだから。
English: Mr. Armstrong says he's not really fond of them.

Japanese: じゃあ、お客さんが 忘れていったのかな。
English: Then maybe one of the customers left it behind?

Japanese: 見てほしいのは、 このラクガキなんだけど‥‥
English: Anyway, what I want you to take a look at is this scribble here.

Japanese: あああああッ！ ‥‥こ、こ、これッス！
English: Ah-ha! That's it, sir!

Japanese: 《クリーニング・ボンバー》‥‥ あのＣＤに書いてあった名前ッス！
English: "MC Bomber"! That was the name that was written on the CD!

Japanese: （やっぱり‥‥）
English: (Just as I thought...)

Japanese: 《くりきんとん》じゃ なかったんだ‥‥
English: I guess it wasn't "MC Screwdriver" after all, huh...

Japanese: じゃあ、１０００万円というのは ヤッパリ‥‥契約金ッスかねえ。
English: So, that $100,000 must be a down payment for a record deal, right?

Japanese: １０００万円もらえるんなら、 あたしだって歌っちゃうよ！
English: If someone gave me $100,000, I'd sing for sure!

Japanese: 《倉院音頭》とか《霊媒おけさ》 とか《マヨイ節》とか！
English: "The Master of Kurain" or "The Spirit Song" or even "Maya's Theme"!

Japanese: それはともかく‥‥、
English: Um, OK, Maya...

Japanese: ‥‥その見本版のＣＤは、 被害者のテーブルの上にあった。
English: So, if the sample CD was on the victim's table...

Japanese: つまり！　この新聞‥‥、 被害者のものかもしれない！
English: That means this newspaper may have belonged to the victim!
You're right!

Japanese: （被害者の、事件当日の 遺留品‥‥か）
English: (So the victim left this behind on the day of the murder, huh...)

Japanese: もっと、ちゃんと 調べられないかな‥‥？
English: I think we better step up the investigation, don't you Nick?

Japanese: あの‥‥これなんですけど。
English: Um, Maggey...

Japanese: あれ。真宵さん、 アルバイト探してるんですか？
English: Oh, are you looking for some part-time work, Maya?

Japanese: あ！　ウチの店長なら きっと、やとってくれるッスよ！
English: Why don't you take a job at the restaurant? I bet Mr. Armstrong would hire you.

Japanese: え。あ、あたしですか？
English: Really? Me?

Japanese: ウエイトレスさん、 けっこう楽しいッスよ。
English: It's pretty cool being a waitress, you know.

Japanese: それに、キッチンには 店長のヒミツがあって。
English: And in the kitchen, you'll get to see all of the chef's greatest secrets!

Japanese: ヒミツ‥‥？
English: What secrets...?

Japanese: コッソリ見てみるといいッス。 ちょっと、オモシロイから。
English: You can't make it obvious, but just spy on him for a bit. You'll see. It's interesting.

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、急に やってみたくなってきた。
English: Wow! I can't wait to get my apron on now!

Japanese: これなんだけど‥‥
English: Maggey... About this...

Japanese: あれ。成歩堂さんも アルバイト探してるんですか？
English: Oh, are you looking for some part-time work too, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: そんなワケないでしょう。
English: Me? No!

Japanese: 成歩堂さんならきっと、 やとってくれるッス。ウチの店長。
English: Because I'm sure Mr. Armstrong would hire you, sir.

Japanese: え。ぼ、ぼくを‥‥？
English: Huh? What? Me?

Japanese: ウエイトレスさん、 けっこう楽しいッスよ。
English: It's pretty cool being a waitress, you know.

Japanese: （‥‥しかも、 ウエイトレスさんかよ！）
English: (I couldn't be a "waitress" even if I tried!)

Japanese: たしかに、このスズキ。 ムカシは婦人警官だったッス。
English: I know I used to be on the police force, sir.

Japanese: でも今は、とらわれの身。 なんの情報も入ってこないッス。
English: But I'm incarcerated, now. So I can't use my connections to help you.

Japanese: 今の自分にわかるのは、 レストランのメニューぐらいッス。
English: All I can help you out with now is the menu at Trés Bien, sir...

Japanese: グチっぽくなっちゃったよ。 マコちゃん。
English: Aww, don't let it get you down, Maggey!

Japanese: 去年は、お世話になりました！
English: Thanks for your help last year!

Japanese: よかったよね！ この前の事件、ムザイになって。
English: Aw, it was nothing. I'm glad we got you off the hook.

Japanese: 今年もよろしくおねがいします！
English: I hope you can help me out this year, too!

Japanese: ‥‥ムザイになるといいね。 今回の事件も。
English: Yeah, me too. It'd be sweet if we could get that verdict overturned.

Japanese: あ‥‥イトノコセンパイ！
English: Oh, Detective Gumshoe!

Japanese: 警官時代は、さんざん メンドウを見てもらったッス！
English: He really looked out for me when I was on the force!

Japanese: 最近は会ってないの？
English: Have you seen him recently?

Japanese: ううん‥‥ウチのレストランには 来てくれないッスねー。
English: No. He's never been to the restaurant.

Japanese: しょうがないかな。 フランス料理だもんね。
English: Guess he's not a fan of French cuisine, huh?

Japanese: イトノコさんの主食は ソーメンだから。
English: He's an instant noodle type of guy, after all.

Japanese: とっても、すずしげッスね！
English: Yeah, he prefers the simple things in life.

Japanese: （まずしげ、とも言う）
English: (...Or he's just simply too poor to afford anything else.)

Japanese: スズキ、どんなコトがあっても、 くじけないッス！
English: No matter what happens, I never let things get me down!

Japanese: いつも前向きだよねー、 マコちゃん。
English: You're always so positive, aren't you, Maggey?

Japanese: “マコ”という名前は、 “マけないコ”というイミッス！
English: Macho Maggey Byrde, they say! The early Byrde catches the worm! A Byrde in the hand!

Japanese: え。そうだったんだ。
English: Wow! All those phrases are named after you?

Japanese: そしてスズキ、どんなコトからも、 逃げないッス！
English: And I never turn my back on anything!

Japanese: いつも強気だしねー、 マコちゃん。
English: You're always so strong, aren't you, Maggey?

Japanese: “マコ”の名前のもう１つのイミは “マっコう勝負”ッス！
English: Macho Maggey Byrde, they say! I'm a tough old Byrde!

Japanese: “マっコう勝負”だって、 なるほどくん。
English: "Tough old byrde", Nick! Hee hee! Do you get it?

Japanese: ‥‥なるほど。
English: ...Yeah, I get it.

Japanese: あ‥‥このヒト！ ウチの常連さんッス！
English: Ah! This man! He comes into the restaurant a lot.

Japanese: 知ってるんだ。
English: So you know him?

Japanese: とってもヤサシイ おじいちゃんッス。
English: Yes, sir. He's a really nice old man.

Japanese: なぜかいつもファーストフードの 割引券を持ち歩いていて‥‥
English: And he always has all these coupons for fast-food joints, for some reason.

Japanese: アタシが料理を持っていくと、 エプロンに挟もうとするッス。
English: He tries to put them into my apron whenever I take his food to him.

Japanese: へええ。
English: Really?

Japanese: （‥‥なんてアブない ジイさんなんだ‥‥）
English: (Sounds like he's more of a creepy old man than a nice one...)

Japanese: あの事件があった日も いたんだよね。お店に。
English: He was there on the day of the murder too, wasn't he?

Japanese: そうらしいッスけど‥‥ スズキは気がつかなかったッス。
English: That's what I heard. But I didn't notice him myself, sir.

Japanese: （‥‥？　そんなに 広かったか？　あの店‥‥）
English: (Huh? It's not like it's that big of a restaurant... is it?)

Japanese: 店長ッス！ ホンドボーさん。
English: Oh, so you met the owner, Mr. Armstrong!

Japanese: なんというか‥‥ 個性的なヒトだね。
English: He's, um... Oh, how can I put this... pretty unique, huh?

Japanese: キョーレツなオーラを感じるッス！ シェフとしても、ヒトとしても。
English: He has a really intense aura. As a chef and as a person.

Japanese: たしかに、感じるなあ。 ‥‥キョーレツなアロマを。
English: Yeah, he has a pretty intense aroma, too.

Japanese: あの、店長さんも 見てないんですか？　ハンニン‥‥
English: So did Mr. Armstrong really not see the killer...?

Japanese: そうみたいッス。
English: Apparently not.

Japanese: まあ‥‥店長は、ずっと キッチンにいましたから‥‥
English: Well, he's in the kitchen all the time, so I guess it's possible that he didn't.

Japanese: あ‥‥そのヒト、 亡くなった被害者ッスね。
English: Ah! That's the victim, isn't it?

Japanese: たしか、『初めて見た』って 言ってたよね。
English: That day was the first time you ever saw this man, right?

Japanese: そッス！　ゼンゼン 知らないヒトッス。
English: Yes. I'd never met or seen him before then.

Japanese: たまたまアタシが コーヒー運んだだけッス！
English: I just happened to be the waitress who served him his coffee, sir. That's all!

Japanese: ‥‥コンピュータの プログラマーらしいけど。
English: It seems he was a computer programmer.

Japanese: もお、そんな！　自分は、 キカイはゼンゼン、ダメッス！
English: Really? I'm useless with computers. Completely hopeless.

Japanese: “ぷらぐろまー”なんて 知り合い、いないッス！
English: I don't know any computer programs.

Japanese: （ビミョーにまちがってるな）
English: (Did she mean "programmers"?)

Japanese: でも‥‥法廷では、動機も 立証されたんでしょ？　きみの。
English: But when you were on trial, they must've established a motive for you, right?

Japanese: アタシが、何か盗んだとか 盗まないとか‥‥
English: They said something about me stealing something. Or was it that I didn't steal...

Japanese: もお、自分にはワケのわからない ハナシだったッス！
English: Oh, I don't know. Even I couldn't follow all the arguments!

Japanese: ‥‥ワケがわからないまま、 有罪になっちゃったの？
English: You couldn't follow the arguments, but you were still found guilty?

Japanese: ニセ成歩堂さんの説明も、 まるっきりイミ不明だったッス。
English: Your phony's statements didn't make a lot of sense either, sir.

Japanese: （それはきっと、ワザとだな） ‥‥しかたない。自分で調べるか。
English: (That must've been his plan.) ...Guess I'll have to look into this myself.

Japanese: たしかに、このスズキ。 ムカシは婦人警官だったッス。
English: I know I used to be on the police force, sir.

Japanese: でも今は、とらわれの身。 なんの情報も入ってこないッス。
English: But I'm incarcerated now. So I can't use my connections to help you.

Japanese: 今の自分にわかるのは、前科者と 店の常連さんだけッス。
English: All I can tell you about now is info about ex-cons or the clientele of Trés Bien, sir...

Japanese: グチっぽくなっちゃったよ。 マコちゃん。
English: Aww, don't let it get you down, Maggey!

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: January 6 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: ‥‥マコちゃんは 取り調べ中らしい。
English: Looks like they have Maggey in questioning.

Japanese: 事件のことは だいたい聞いたと思うけど‥‥
English: I guess I've asked her pretty much everything...

Japanese: もう一度、聞きたくなったら またあとで来るか‥‥
English: I'll come back if there's anything else I need to ask her later.

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitors the visitor's room.

Japanese: 今は、することがないのだろう。 立ったまま寝ている。
English: There's nothing for him to do right now, so he's catching some vertical z's.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 警察署・刑事課
English: January 6 Police Station Criminal Affairs Dept.

Japanese: うーん、ひさしぶりだなあ。 警察署に来るのも。
English: It's been ages since we came down to the precinct, huh, Nick?

Japanese: ‥‥イトノコ刑事、 いないみたいだ。
English: Looks like Gumshoe isn't around.

Japanese: ヒトをはたらかせておいて、 イイ気なもんだね。
English: He's got it so easy, leaving everyone else to do the work!

Japanese: いやいや。裁判所やら何やら、 動き回ってるんじゃないかな。
English: No, he's out there somewhere. My bet is on the courthouse.

Japanese: この事件の再審理をするために。
English: He's probably trying to arrange the retrial of this case.

Japanese: やれやれ。先に留置所で ハンニンの話を聞こうか。
English: Guess that means we should go to the detention center and chat with our killer, huh?

Japanese: ハンニンって言うなよ‥‥。
English: After being convicted without a fair trial, I'm not sure killer is the right label...

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 警察署・刑事課
English: January 6 Police Station Criminal Affairs Dept.

Japanese: ‥‥イトノコ刑事、 いないみたいだ。
English: Looks like Gumshoe isn't around.

Japanese: きっと、この事件を 再審理させるために‥‥、
English: I guess he's still trying to organize the retrial.

Japanese: 裁判所やら何やら、 動き回っているんだろう。
English: He's probably at the courthouse or something.

Japanese: ‥‥また、あとで来てみるか‥‥
English: I'll catch up with him later.

Japanese: あ、カワイイ！　これって、 ケーサツのマスコットだよね。
English: Hey, that's the police mascot, isn't it? It's so cute!

Japanese: “タイホくん”だよ。
English: That's the "Blue Badger".

Japanese: ワシが考えて、ワシが作った。 今や、この国の警察のシンボルさ。
English: It was my idea! I made it! And now it's the national symbol of the police force!

Japanese: １ぴき、ふえてますね。 ‥‥ピンクのが。
English: So what's with the pink one? It's new, right?

Japanese: “１ぴき”って言わない、 なるほどくん。
English: She's not an "it", Nick. She's a "she", right Chief?

Japanese: それは、新しい仲間の “タイホちゃん”だよ。
English: Yup. Meet the "Pink Badger".

Japanese: あれ。 名前、おんなじなんですか？
English: So one's called "Blue", and the other's called "Pink", but they're both called "Badger"?

Japanese: ケッコンしてるからね、 その２人は。
English: You got it! They're married.

Japanese: あの。タイホくんとタイホちゃん ‥‥手錠でつながれてますけど。
English: Um... Mr. & Mrs. Badger seem to be cuffed together, Chief.

Japanese: ‥‥それが ケッコンというヤツだからねえ。
English: You got it! That's marriage for you!

Japanese: （さすが警察のマスコット‥‥ 重たいな、なんとなく）
English: (Whoa... A mascot that's deeper than the deepest of deep oceans...)

Japanese: いつ来ても置いてあるな ‥‥このマスコット。
English: No matter what, whenever I come here, that mascot's there to greet me.

Japanese: “タイホくん”だよ。
English: You got that right. That's the "Blue Badger".

Japanese: ワシが考えて、ワシが作った。 今や、この国の警察のシンボルさ。
English: It was my idea! I made it! And now it's the national symbol of the police force!

Japanese: １ぴき、ふえてますね。 ‥‥ピンクのが。
English: So what's with the pink one? It's new, right?

Japanese: それは、新しい仲間の “タイホちゃん”だよ。
English: Yup. Meet the "Pink Badger".

Japanese: あれ。 名前、おんなじなんですか？
English: So one's called "Blue", and the other's called "Pink", but they're both called "Badger"?

Japanese: ケッコンしてるからね、 その２人は。
English: You got it! They're married.

Japanese: （次に来たときは ムスコが生まれてそうだな‥‥）
English: (So should I expect to see Baby Badger next time I'm here...?)

Japanese: 刑事さんだろうか。
English: That must be one of the detectives.

Japanese: ‥‥何やら、ひとりでぶつぶつ つぶやいている。
English: He's mumbling something to himself.

Japanese: “赤信号　ムシして死ぬな ヒトとして死ね”
English: "Don't die running a red light. Die the old-fashioned way -- of old age!"

Japanese: ‥‥キマリだな。ちょっと ユーモラスなのがいい。
English: We have a winner! Inject a bit of humor, that's the key.

Japanese: 交通安全キャンペーンの 標語を考えているようだ。
English: He must be coming up with slogans for a safety campaign. ...I guess?

Japanese: 刑事課の課長さんだろう。
English: This must be the chief of the detectives here.

Japanese: ‥‥パソコンの画面を、 食いいるように見つめている。
English: He's glued to his computer screen.

Japanese: なにぃ‥‥！　怪人☆仮面マスク、 銀行にサッソウと現れて‥‥
English: WHAT!? Mask☆DeMasque turned up at the bank? What's he doing!?

Japanese: “お手がら！　強盗をタイホ” ‥‥だと！
English: "Hands up, you scalawags!" "You're under arrest!" Hey! That's our job!

Japanese: （優作くんたち‥‥新しい道を 歩き出したみたいだな）
English: (Looks like Ron DeLite's opted for a new career.)

Japanese: カチョー！　インターネットで 遊ばないでください！
English: Quit surfing the net, Chief!

Japanese: す‥‥スマン。ゴメン。
English: S-Sorry... I was just, um...

Japanese: （なんか、ピリピリしてるな。 空気が‥‥）
English: (Things feel pretty tense in here...)

Japanese: 婦人警官がビシッと 敬礼している。
English: A poster of a female police officer...

Japanese: ‥‥と思ったら、制服を着た アイドルのグラビアだった。
English: Wait, no. That's the latest "Babes in Uniform" calendar. My bad.

Japanese: 刑事たちの机がならんでいる。
English: These are the detectives' desks.

Japanese: パソコンに、資料。 ‥‥思ったより片づいているな。
English: There are computers and files on each one. Funny, they're a lot tidier than I expected.

Japanese: たぶん、あまり席にいることが ないんだろう。
English: I guess the detectives don't spend a lot of time at their desks.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 警察署・刑事課
English: January 6 Police Station Criminal Affairs Dept.

Japanese: どッスか！　見つかったッスか！ ムザイを立証する証拠は！
English: Well, pal!? Have you found the evidence yet!? The one that's gonna find her innocent!?

Japanese: い、いやいや。まだ、 始めたばっかりですから。調査。
English: Um, no. Not yet. We've only just started our investigation.

Japanese: いッスか！　知ってる情報なら、 じゃんじゃんタレ流すッス！
English: Well, whatever you need to know, I'll give you the dirt on it.

Japanese: 今、担当してる事件も サボりまくりッス！
English: I'm putting off all my other cases for now, pal!

Japanese: （そうとうアツくなってるな ‥‥イトノコ刑事‥‥）
English: (Gumshoe's really fired up about this...)

Japanese: あ！　それから！
English: Oh, yeah! One more thing!

Japanese: 再審が、正式に決まったッス！ 開廷は明日の１０時。
English: The retrial's been approved. Court's sitting at 10 AM tomorrow.

Japanese: 担当は、あのゴドー検事ッス！
English: And Godot's gonna be the prosecutor...

Japanese: （うう。アイツか‥‥）
English: (Oh. Him.)

Japanese: とにかく！　もし、また マコクンを有罪にしたら‥‥
English: Now, listen up, pal! If Maggey's found guilty again...

Japanese: ゆ、有罪にしたら？
English: Y-Yes...?

Japanese: その‥‥‥ もー、アレッス！　タイホッス！
English: Um... I'll... I'll make sure you get locked up good for it, got it!?

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 警察署・刑事課
English: January 6 Police Station Criminal Affairs Dept.

Japanese: お。ちょうどよかったッス！
English: Hey, you're just in time!

Japanese: なんですか？
English: What is it, Detective Gumshoe?

Japanese: アンタからあずかっていた新聞、 ブンセキが終わったッス。
English: The lab got back to me about that newspaper you gave me.

Japanese: （ラクガキの筆跡‥‥）
English: (He must mean the sports paper with the memo scribbled on it...)

Japanese: そ、それで‥‥どうでした？ 検査の結果は！
English: So? What did they say? Did the analysis turn up anything?

Japanese: どうやら‥‥被害者・岡　高夫が 書いたと見て、まちがいないッス。
English: They said the doodle was written by the victim, Glen Elg. No doubt about it.

Japanese: （やっぱり‥‥そうだったのか）
English: (I expected as much...)

Japanese: このスポーツ新聞は事件当日、 被害者が店に持ちこんだ‥‥
English: The victim took the paper with him to the restaurant on the day of the murder.

Japanese: そう考えていいと思うッス。
English: That's our best interpretation of the facts at the moment.

Japanese: 証拠品《スポーツ新聞》のデータを もう一度、ファイルした。
English: Sports Paper refiled into the Court Record.

Japanese: でも‥‥やっぱり、 どこかで聞いたコト、あるッス。
English: "MC Bomber"...

Japanese: 《クリーニング・ボンバー》‥‥
English: I get the feeling I've heard that name somewhere before...

Japanese: ‥‥ま。とにかく。
English: Oh, well. I guess it'll come back to me.

Japanese: あのレストランで見つけたモノは、 ちゃーんと自分に教えるッスよ。
English: Don't forget to report back to me with whatever you find in the restaurant, OK, pal?

Japanese: （‥‥エラそうだな。でも‥‥）
English: (Since when did I start taking orders from Gumshoe...? Although...)

Japanese: （なんか、見せるべきモノが あるような気もするな‥‥）
English: (I get the feeling there's something I need to show him...)

Japanese: 有罪になっちゃったの‥‥ 須々木　マコさんだったんですね。
English: So the guilty party was Maggey Byrde, huh?

Japanese: そ‥‥そッスよ。
English: Yeah...

Japanese: 彼女、もとは婦人警官で‥‥
English: Back when she was on the police force...

Japanese: 新人のとき、イトノコ刑事が メンドウを見てあげたんですよね。
English: ...you were her mentor when she was a rookie, right?

Japanese: まま、まあ。あ、当たらずとも 遠からず、という感じッスね。
English: Yeah, I kept a close eye on her. I-I mean, not too close. You know.
‥‥‥‥‥‥<Phoenix>

Japanese: な、なんスかァ、その目は！
English: Hey, what's with the funny looks, pal?

Japanese: じじ、自分はベツにアレッス！ そんなんじゃないッス！
English: I was just her... It wasn't anything like...

Japanese: たしかに、マコクンは研修時代、 自分のブカだったッスが‥‥
English: Look! Sure, I was her boss when she was doing her training.

Japanese: それだけッス！ やましいトコロはないッス！
English: But that was it. Nothing happened.

Japanese: （イトノコ刑事、ミョーに必死に なってると思ったら‥‥）
English: (Gumshoe sure is sweating up a storm over nothing...)

Japanese: （そういうコトだったのか）
English: (...Ah, so that's it! Our big ol' Gumshoe has a little ol' crush on Maggey!)

Japanese: そ‥‥そーゆうコトって どーゆうコトッスかッ！ I...
English: I don't like her or anything!

Japanese: 自分は‥‥自分はああアァァァッ！ I...
English: I was... Aaaargh!

Japanese: （あとで真宵ちゃんに 教えてあげないとな）
English: (Note to self: Gossip with Maya about this later!)

Japanese: だ、ダメッス！ ゼッタイ、ヒミツッスよ！
English: Look, pal! Don't tell anyone, OK!? You've gotta keep it a secret, got it!?

Japanese: それはいいですけど‥‥
English: S-Sure...

Japanese: ヒトのココロの中を 読まないでもらえますか？
English: And would you mind not guessing what I'm thinking all the time?

Japanese: アンタの目は、 口よりもモノを言うッス！
English: Hey, tell your face, pal, not me. You'd have to be blind not to see what's on your mind.

Japanese: 捜査といっても、 動いてるのは自分だけッス。
English: This isn't really a proper investigation. I'm kind of working on it by myself.

Japanese: え。そうなんですか‥‥
English: O-Oh, that's right...

Japanese: 一度は有罪が決まった事件ッス。 証拠はカンペキにそろってるッス。
English: The judge already ruled on the case, and all the evidence is in already.

Japanese: ひたすら、マコクンの証言だけが ジジツと食いちがってるッス。
English: The only problem is with Maggey's testimony.

Japanese: うう。イヤな感じですね‥‥
English: Yeah... Doesn't sound very good for us, huh?

Japanese: 自分も、明日の手続きに追われて、 事件の資料を見る時間もないッス！
English: Look, pal. I've got a mountain of papers on this case to look over before tomorrow.

Japanese: ただ‥‥これだけは 言っておきたいッス！
English: So I'm just gonna say this...

Japanese: マコクンは、ゼッタイ ウソはついていないッス！
English: Maggey Byrde's no liar.

Japanese: あのコは‥‥あのコは‥‥
English: She's... She's...

Japanese: そそっかしく、思いちがいと カンちがいがトレードマークの、
English: OK, so she's a bit out there, and a bit off base sometimes.

Japanese: オチャメな婦警さんッス！
English: But she was a good cop.

Japanese: （ゼンゼン たよりにならないじゃないか！）
English: (That's not exactly complimentary, you know!)

Japanese: それにしても‥‥どれぐらい 食いちがってるんですか？
English: So what do you think really happened?

Japanese: マコちゃんの言ってるコトと、 その‥‥ジジツは？
English: And just how contradictory is her testimony?

Japanese: 一番キョーレツなのが、 テーブルにいた、人数ッス。
English: The biggest problem with Maggey's testimony is the number of people at the table.

Japanese: ああ‥‥たしかに。
English: Yeah. My thoughts exactly.

Japanese: マコちゃんは、被害者の他に もうヒトリいたと主張してますね。
English: Maggey still insists there was another guy sitting with the victim.

Japanese: ‥‥そッス。しかし‥‥
English: Right. But get this...

Japanese: シェフや、店の客は‥‥ そんなヤツ、見てないッス。
English: Everyone else in the place says the guy was alone. Even the chef.

Japanese: そして、もう１つ。 ＣＤのモンダイがあるッス。
English: And then there's that CD...

Japanese: （ＣＤ‥‥か。そういえば 彼女、言ってたな‥‥）
English: (CD? Oh, yeah. She did mention something about a CD...)

Japanese: テーブルの上には、見本のＣＤが 置いてあったッス。
English: And there was a sample CD on the table, sir.

Japanese: でも‥‥そんなＣＤ、 どこにもなかったッス。
English: But our guys turned that place upside down. There was no CD.
What?

Japanese: 被害者のテーブルどころか、 店の中には存在しなかったッス。
English: Not on the table. Not anywhere in the whole restaurant, pal.

Japanese: で、でも‥‥被害者は、 イヤホンをしていた、とか‥‥
English: But didn't Maggey say the victim was wearing an earpiece, too?

Japanese: 被害者の岡　高夫のムネには、 小型のラジオが入っていたッス。
English: Yeah, but that was for the portable radio in the front pocket of his hoodie.

Japanese: ラジオ‥‥
English: A radio? He didn't have a CD player?

Japanese: この食いちがいを‥‥アンタの ニセモノは説明できなかったッス。
English: You got it. Your phony never explained that contradiction at all.

Japanese: （そういえば‥‥）
English: (Come to think of it...)

Japanese: （店長さん、ＣＤのことは 何も言ってなかったな‥‥）
English: (The owner of Trés Bien didn't mention that CD either.)

Japanese: （あのヒトも、ナニか ヒミツがありそうだよな‥‥）
English: (I don't know why, but I get the feeling Mr. Armstrong's got something to hide...)

Japanese: そういえば‥‥ 被害者のことなんですけど。
English: So, I was wondering...

Japanese: いったい、どんなヒトなんですか？
English: Could you fill me in on the victim?

Japanese: ‥‥岡　高夫（おか　たかお）。 コンピュータ・プログラマーッス。
English: Glen Elg, he was a computer programmer.

Japanese: プログラマー‥‥
English: I see. A programmer...

Japanese: 小さなコンピュータ会社で はたらく、フツーのオトコッス。
English: He was just a regular Joe working for a small-time computer firm.

Japanese: マコクンと、あらゆるイミで 接点はないッス。
English: Maggey never had any contact with the guy before that day.

Japanese: ‥‥ただ、１つ。あの日たまたま、 コーヒーを運んだ、という以外は。
English: ...And all she did was take him his coffee on the day of the murder, pal.

Japanese: （マコちゃんも“見たことない” って言ってたっけな‥‥）
English: (Yeah, Maggey also claimed to have never seen the guy before...)

Japanese: 被害者は、あのレストランを よく利用していたんですか？
English: Did the victim go to the restaurant often?

Japanese: シェフのハナシでは‥‥ 初めての客だったらしいッス。
English: Not according to the chef. Said it was the first time he'd seen the guy.

Japanese: （プログラマー‥‥ 初対面の客、か‥‥）
English: (A programmer... and a first- time customer...)

Japanese: ‥‥そんなオトコを殺害する 理由なんて、ないじゃないですか。
English: What possible reason could Maggey have had to kill a guy like that?

Japanese: 自分もそう思うッスが‥‥
English: That's what I thought.

Japanese: 法廷では、キチンと立証された らしいッス。‥‥殺害の動機も。
English: But a motive was still somehow established in her trial.

Japanese: え！　なんですか？ マコちゃんの“動機”って！
English: You're kidding! What was her supposed "motive"?

Japanese: すまねッスが‥‥自分も忙しくて、 資料にあたる時間がないッス。
English: Sorry, pal. I'm real busy. I haven't even got enough time to sift through these papers.

Japanese: 自分で調べてほしいッス。
English: Look into it yourself, OK?

Japanese: （マコちゃんの“動機”か‥‥）
English: (What could this motive have been...)

Japanese: なんですか？　あのシェフの “気になる点”って‥‥
English: So what exactly is it that caught your attention about the chef at Trés Bien?

Japanese: それが‥‥ちょっと、 言いにくいッス。
English: It's, um... kind of hard to say...

Japanese: あのオッサン、事件にカンケイ ないかもしれないし‥‥。
English: The guy's probably not even connected with the case anyway...

Japanese: まあまあ。じゃんじゃんタレ流す ヤクソクじゃないですか。情報。
English: Hey, come on, Detective. Didn't you say you'd give me the dirt on anything?

Japanese: ビミョーな 個人情報ッスからねえ‥‥。
English: Well, this sort of stuff is kinda... unimportant, gossipy stuff, you know pal?

Japanese: じゃ、こうするッス！
English: Look, how about this?

Japanese: 自分のかわりに、ヤツと吐麗美庵を もっと調べるッス。
English: You go to Trés Bien and investigate the place yourself.

Japanese: それで、アイツがアヤシイと いうことになれば‥‥教えるッス。
English: And if you find out anything suspicious about the guy... you report back to me, OK?

Japanese: うう‥‥しかたありませんね。
English: Um... Don't suppose I get a choice in this, huh?

Japanese: （シェフと吐麗美庵の情報は、 イトノコ刑事に報告するか‥‥）
English: (Guess I better find out more about the chef and Trés Bien; then report back to Gumshoe.)

Japanese: それで‥‥なんですか？ 本土坊さんの“ヒミツ”って。
English: So what's Mr. Armstrong's secret?

Japanese: アンタ、吐麗美庵の ランチは食べたッスか？
English: You ever had lunch at Trés Bien, pal?

Japanese: ‥‥え、ええ。
English: Um... Yes...

Japanese: どうだったッス？
English: So, how was it?

Japanese: あくまでも、いいイミで おいしくありませんでした。
English: To put it nicely... it was inedible.

Japanese: ベツに、この自分に 気をつかわなくていいッス。
English: Hey, don't worry about being nice around me, pal.

Japanese: 店はキレイだし、ウエイトレスは マコクンッスが‥‥
English: You and I both know the reason that place is so empty is because of the food.

Japanese: あの料理のせいで、吐麗美庵は まったく客が入ってないッス。
English: I mean, the place is clean, and he's got a girl like Maggey as a waitress, so...

Japanese: まあ‥‥そうかもしれませんね。
English: Yeah... I guess it's probably the food.

Japanese: トーゼン、あの男は 借金まみれッス。
English: The real scoop on the guy is he's up to his ears in debt.

Japanese: ‥‥どれぐらい まみれてるんですか？
English: Really? How much does he owe?

Japanese: ヤツの借用書のコピーッス。 およそ、５０００万ッス。
English: This is a copy of his loan contract. He's about half a million in the red.

Japanese: ごご、５０００万‥‥って、 “円”ですか？
English: H-Half a million? Are we talking dollars?

Japanese: そりゃそッス。“ドル”だったら とんでもないことに。
English: Yeah. Hey, if it was Sterling, he'd really be in trouble!

Japanese: す、すみません。あまりの金額に、 アタマと声がウラ返っちゃって。
English: Sorry. That figure just took me by surprise.

Japanese: ‥‥どうも、この事件には “ウラ”がありそうッス。
English: Yeah, this case is full of surprises.

Japanese: そして、その“ウラ”には、 このシェフがカラんでいる‥‥
English: And I'd be willing to bet that chef's got something to do with most of them.

Japanese: そんな気がするッス！
English: That's my hunch.

Japanese: 《本土坊の借用書》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Jean's Loan Contract added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ホンモノッスか？　それ。
English: Is that thing real, pal?

Japanese: ‥‥見せると みんなにそう言われます。
English: Why does everyone keep asking me that?

Japanese: どんなヤツッスかねー、 アンタのニセモノ‥‥
English: I wonder what this phony of yours is like...

Japanese: マコちゃんを、ワザと 有罪にしたヤツですよ。
English: He had Maggey found guilty of murder. Doesn't that tell you?

Japanese: （なんとかして、 見つけだしたいな‥‥）
English: (I've got to track this creep down!)

Japanese: 自分も、その裁判は 傍聴していないッスが‥‥
English: I wasn't at the trial myself.

Japanese: 知り合いの刑事に聞いてみたッス。 このときの、アンタの弁護のコト。
English: But I asked this one detective I know how your defense was.

Japanese: ど、どんな感じでしたか？
English: And what did he say?

Japanese: 『あきれてモノも言えないよ』 ‥‥と、前置きしてから‥‥
English: He started off by saying, "I'm at a complete loss for words..."

Japanese: １時間ぐらい聞かされたッス。 アンタの無能ぶりを。
English: But he must have found some quick because he went on about how bad you were for an hour.

Japanese: うう‥‥
English: But...

Japanese: どう見ても、ワザと 有罪にしたんじゃないか、って。
English: He said you sucked so much, it seemed like you were trying to get Maggey found guilty!

Japanese: （ワザと‥‥か‥‥）
English: (It looked like I was "trying to get Maggey found guilty"...?)

Japanese: なんスか？　スポーツ新聞‥‥
English: What's that? A sports paper?

Japanese: 吐麗美庵のマガジンラックで 見つけたんですよ。
English: Yeah. I found it in the magazine rack at Trés Bien.

Japanese: 日付が‥‥事件当日ッスか。 ちょっと、イミありげッス‥‥
English: It's dated the same day as the murder. You may be onto something here.

Japanese: ‥‥ホラ。ここに、 ラクガキがありますよね？
English: And take a look at this. See this writing here?

Japanese: “くりーにんぐ・ぼんばー” ‥‥‥‥‥‥はて。
English: "MC Bomber"... Hey!

Japanese: どうかしましたか？
English: What is it?

Japanese: どこかで聞いたコトがあるッス。 《クリーニング・ボンバー》‥‥
English: I've heard that name somewhere before. Yeah, "MC Bomber"...

Japanese: （‥‥いつになく、マジメに 考えこんでいる‥‥）
English: (Wow, he actually seems to be thinking for once!)

Japanese: ダメ。思い出せないッス。
English: Ack, it's no good. I can't remember...

Japanese: （‥‥いつもどおり、ダメだった）
English: (...And he goes back to being the Gumshoe we all know and love.)

Japanese: アンタ！　ちょっとこの新聞、 貸してもらえないッスか？
English: Hey, pal. I'm gonna borrow this paper for a bit, OK?

Japanese: ‥‥このラクガキの 筆跡を調べてみたいッス。
English: I wanna get a handwriting analysis done on this scribble.

Japanese: （筆跡‥‥か。それは、 ぼくも知りたいな‥‥）
English: (Handwriting, huh? It'd be good to know more about that, in any case...)

Japanese: これは‥‥オモシロイ 手がかりになるかもしれないッス！
English: Thanks, pal! I bet this'll turn out to be an interesting clue!

Japanese: 証拠品《スポーツ新聞》を イトノコ刑事にあずけた。
English: Sports Paper given to Detective Gumshoe.

Japanese: お。またもらってきたッスか？ ニオイつきの小ビン。
English: You got one of those aroma bottles too, huh?

Japanese: それが‥‥この小ビン、 なんのニオイもしないんですよ。
English: Only this one doesn't smell.

Japanese: ‥‥イミがわかんねッス。
English: Huh? I don't get you...

Japanese: アロマテラピーのボトルに まじって、１本だけ、これが。
English: This was mixed in with all the other aromatherapy bottles, but it's not the same.

Japanese: デザインもゼンゼン ちがいますよね‥‥？
English: It doesn't even look the same, wouldn't you agree?

Japanese: ニオわない小ビン‥‥ それはかえって、ニオうッス！
English: A cologne bottle that doesn't smell, huh? Smells like a skunk to me, pal!

Japanese: 今度は、ちょっとその小ビン、 貸してもらえないッスか？
English: Mind letting me borrow that bottle for a while?

Japanese: 中身をブンセキしてみたいッス。
English: I wanna send it to the lab for analysis.

Japanese: （被害者は“毒殺”された。 ‥‥ビンの中身は重要だな）
English: (The victim was poisoned, so the contents of this bottle are pretty important...)

Japanese: 証拠品《小ビン》を イトノコ刑事にあずけた。
English: Small Bottle given to Detective Gumshoe.

Japanese: ‥‥やっぱり‥‥ あのシェフはアヤしいッスね。
English: I had a hunch there was something funny about that chef.

Japanese: 本土坊　薫さん‥‥ですか。
English: You suspect Jean Armstrong?

Japanese: ジツは‥‥自分は、 ヤツのヒミツをつかんでいるッス。
English: I've got that guy's number. I know what his secret is.

Japanese: （そういえば、そんなコトを 言ってたな‥‥イトノコ刑事）
English: (That must be the same secret Gumshoe was talking about before.)

Japanese: どうやら、アンタには 話しておいたほうがいいッスね。
English: I guess I'd better fill you in on the details.

Japanese: 本土坊のヒミツを‥‥。
English: About this Armstrong guy's secret, I mean...

Japanese: このラクガキは、 おそらく被害者が書いたものッス。
English: So according to the boys down at the lab, the doodles were probably made by the victim.

Japanese: “おそらく”‥‥ですか。
English: Probably?

Japanese: サインペンで書かれたサンプルは 鑑定がムズカシイッスから。
English: Analyzing this kind of writing is difficult since it's done with a fiber-tipped pen.

Japanese: でも、だいじょぶッス！ この自分がタイコ判を押すッス！
English: But don't worry! I'll vouch for it.

Japanese: （なんでイトノコ刑事が 押すんだ？　タイコ判‥‥）
English: (Why would Gumshoe have to vouch for it? Can't it stand on its own?)

Japanese: ５０００万円ッスよ！
English: It's for half a million dollars, pal!

Japanese: １円玉で、えーと‥‥ ざっと５０００万枚ッス！
English: That's... um... half a million dollar bills!

Japanese: （今の“えーと”は なんだったんだ‥‥？）
English: (What was the "um" in there for!?)

Japanese: 今回の事件、そんな 大金がカラんでいるんですか？
English: Is there really that much money tied up in this case?

Japanese: 資料を見てないから 知らないッス。
English: I can't give you an answer on that, pal. Not without the case file in front of me.

Japanese: でも‥‥
English: But I'll tell you this...

Japanese: 本土坊が、金のためなら なんでもやる状況にいたコトは、
English: That Armstrong guy would've done anything for cash.

Japanese: やぶさかでないッス。
English: He was desperate, you know?

Japanese: ‥‥よくわからないけど、 なんとなくわかりました。
English: No, I don't. But I think I sort of get the picture.

Japanese: アレ。今日は いっしょじゃないッスか。
English: Hey! So how come she's not with you today?

Japanese: ええ。今、レストランで はたらいています。
English: She's working down at the restaurant right now.

Japanese: ‥‥そッスか。 カナしいッスよね‥‥ビンボーは。
English: Working, huh? Yeah, it's tough being broke. I would know.

Japanese: 今度、おいしいソーメンの ゆで方を教えてあげるッス。
English: Next time I see her, I'll show her how to make the world's best instant noodles.

Japanese: ビンボーどうし、 強く生きるッス！
English: We low earners have to stick together! It's the only way!

Japanese: （なんでアクシュしなくちゃ ならないんだ‥‥？）
English: (Um... Why is he shaking my hand with such enthusiasm...?)

Japanese: あいかわらず、元気そうッスね。
English: You're looking upbeat, as usual.

Japanese: イトノコさんも、あいかわらず つかれたカオしてるッスね！
English: And you're looking tired as usual, Detective Gumshoe!

Japanese: 同じニンゲンなのに、 この差はいったい、なんなんス‥‥
English: Yeah. That's life, huh? One guy's got it all, and one guy's got squat.

Japanese: あれ。ボヤき始めたよ。
English: Is it just me or did the room get darker all of a sudden...?

Japanese: ま。ま。きっと、今年は イトノコさんの年になりますよ！
English: You know, Detective. This is the year of the, um... gum!

Japanese: そ‥‥そッスかねえ。
English: I-It is?

Japanese: ええ。たぶん。
English: Yeah. ...I think.

Japanese: そ‥‥そッスよね！
English: Gum, huh? This is gonna be a great year for this ol' Gumshoe then, right?

Japanese: あは。元気になったよ。
English: Look at that. He perked up in no time!

Japanese: 遊ぶなよ‥‥
English: Maya... Don't mess with him like that.

Japanese: 面会には、行ってあげましたか？
English: Have you gone to see Maggey?

Japanese: モチロンッス！ ただ‥‥
English: Of course I have! But I...

Japanese: あまりのコトに、なぐさめる コトバが出て来なかったッス。
English: I wasn't much good at consoling her. I'm... I'm not very good with words.

Japanese: そう‥‥ですよね。
English: Oh...

Japanese: 思わずうつむいたら、マコクンが はげましてくれたッス。
English: Yeah. I guess I must've looked a bit down. Maggey was really supportive of me.

Japanese: ‥‥うれしかったッス。
English: It was great to have someone to talk to.

Japanese: （何しに行ったんだ、 この刑事さんは‥‥）
English: (Did he go for her, or for himself...?)

Japanese: ‥‥自分が情けないッス。
English: I'm such a loser.

Japanese: マコクンを、一人前の刑事に するのがユメだったのに‥‥
English: I had high hopes for Maggey. I was gonna make her the best detective there ever was.

Japanese: いつのまにか、殺人の罪を 着せられて、タイホされて‥‥
English: But then all of a sudden she was charged with murder and arrested.

Japanese: 有罪判決が下されるまで、 気がつかなかったなんて！
English: I never saw it coming. I never imagined they'd find her guilty.

Japanese: ‥‥もう、自分が イヤになったッス。
English: I hate myself for not being able to do anything.

Japanese: 大丈夫ですよ、イトノコ刑事。
English: It's OK, Detective Gumshoe.

Japanese: ‥‥まだ、最後のチャンスが 残っています。
English: We still have a chance to make this right.

Japanese: アンタが、ココロなしか いいヤツに見えてきたッス。
English: You know what? You're not such a bad guy after all, pal.

Japanese: ‥‥ガンコなジイさんッス。
English: He looks like one of those grouchy old man types.

Japanese: そうですね。
English: Yeah.

Japanese: 自分は、ガンコなジイさんは キライじゃないッス。
English: It's OK, though. I don't mind guys like that.

Japanese: でも、事件の関係者となれば、 ベツッス。
English: But, if he's involved with this case somehow, that's a different story.

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。
English: Um... sure.

Japanese: あのジイさんから情報を 引きずり出すには‥‥
English: Here's a tip for you, pal. If you wanna get information out of a guy like that...

Japanese: “弱点”を探し出して、 そこをたたくしかないッスね‥‥
English: ...you're gonna have to find his weakness and try to get under his skin.

Japanese: （あのジイさんの “弱点”‥‥ねえ‥‥）
English: (His weakness? I wonder what that might be...)

Japanese: 吐麗美庵のシェフ、ッスか。
English: The chef of Trés Bien, huh?

Japanese: 会うなり『アナタ、カラダが毒素で パンパンね』なんて言われて‥‥
English: You know what that chef said to me? "Oh là là! Your body is full of la toxins."

Japanese: このビンをわたされたッス。
English: And then he gave me this bottle.

Japanese: なんですか、それ。
English: What's in it?

Japanese: ラベルには“ジュニパー”って 書いてあるッス。
English: I don't know. The label says, "Juniper".

Japanese: おフロにちょっと しのばせろ、と要求されたッス。
English: I'm under orders to put a few drops of it in my bath every day.

Japanese: はあ‥‥要求されましたか。
English: Under orders?

Japanese: じつは、彼女‥‥いや。カレには ちょっと引っかかる点があるッス。
English: Yeah. You know, there's something about that lady... I mean, guy.

Japanese: ひ、ひかれる点‥‥？
English: Huh!? You can't stop thinking about him!?

Japanese: ちち、チガうッス！　なんであんな 中年オトコにひかれるッスか！
English: Not like that, pal! Gimme a break! He's not my type.

Japanese: “引っかかる点”ッス！
English: I mean, I can't stop thinking that he's involved with this case somehow!

Japanese: （詳しく聞いたほうがいいな‥‥）
English: (Sounds like he knows a little something more about our charming chef.)

Japanese: 明日、マコクンの再審理を 担当するッス。
English: That's the guy who's gonna be the prosecutor in Maggey's retrial tomorrow.

Japanese: そ、そうなんですか。
English: O-Oh, really?

Japanese: 他に、もっと大きな事件も あるのに‥‥
English: He was working on a bunch of more important cases at the moment...

Japanese: なぜか、アンタと戦うために キャンセルしてるッス。
English: ...but he canceled them all just so he could take you on, pal.

Japanese: （‥‥なぜ、そこまでして ぼくを倒そうとするんだ‥‥？）
English: (Why's that guy so determined to see me fail?)

Japanese: ホント、人気者ッスねー。 ‥‥モチロン、悪いイミで。
English: You sure attract a lot of attention, huh? Too bad it's all the wrong kind.

Japanese: ムカシ‥‥一度だけ、 尋問を受けたことがあるッス。
English: She cross-examined me once, you know.

Japanese: え‥‥千尋さんに、ですか？
English: What? Mia did?

Japanese: あれは‥‥名勝負だったッス。
English: Yeah. That was us. The big guns locking horns.

Japanese: 証人対弁護士！　アツいアツい カケヒキが展開されたッス。
English: Witness versus lawyer! It was a battle of cunning tactics and tricks.

Japanese: （証人がカケヒキしちゃ マズいだろ‥‥）
English: (But the witness isn't supposed to play any tricks...)

Japanese: おしいヒトを亡くしたッス‥‥。
English: I can't believe she's gone...

Japanese: あの。これなんですけど‥‥
English: What do you make of this...?

Japanese: 今は‥‥マコクンのコトで アタマがいっぱいッス。
English: Sorry, pal. All I can think about is Maggey at the moment.
‥‥‥‥‥‥<Phoenix>

Japanese: ちち、ちがうッス！　そういう イミじゃなくて、自分は‥‥！
English: N-No! I didn't mean it like that! I meant...

Japanese: （ムリしなくていいのに‥‥）
English: (You don't have to explain... Really...)

Japanese: 刑事さんだろうか。
English: That must be one of the detectives.

Japanese: ‥‥何やら、ひとりでぶつぶつ つぶやいている。
English: He's mumbling something to himself.

Japanese: “アクセルを　ふめば人生 早送り”‥‥
English: "Hitting the gas is like hitting the fast-forward button on your life."

Japanese: ‥‥わかりにくいな。 『すぐ死ぬよ』ってコトなんだが。
English: No. That's too obscure. You don't really get the "you'll die before your time" idea.

Japanese: いっそ、もっとストレートで、 明るく楽しい感じがイイか。
English: I need something more direct. Something a bit more light- hearted.

Japanese: “スピード出して 天国へ行こう！”‥‥みたいな。
English: Something like, "Hitting the gas is your passport to paradise!"

Japanese: 交通安全キャンペーンの 標語を考えているようだ。
English: He must be coming up with slogans for a safety campaign. ...I guess?

Japanese: 刑事課の課長さんだろう。
English: This must be the chief of the detectives here.

Japanese: ‥‥パソコンの画面を、 食いいるように見つめている。
English: He's glued to his computer screen.

Japanese: なにぃ‥‥！　毒入りコーヒーの ウエイトレス、再審が決定‥‥
English: WHAT!? Poisoned coffee waitress gets a retrial!?

Japanese: そういえば、今日のワシの コーヒー、イヤにニガいな‥‥。
English: I knew it! I knew there was something funny about the taste of this coffee!

Japanese: ちょっと！　ワシのコーヒー いれたヒト、手をあげて！
English: OK! Who brought me this coffee today? Hands up!

Japanese: （ブカを信用してやれよ！）
English: (Yeesh! Have a little faith in your subordinates!)

Japanese: カチョー！　インターネットで 遊ばないでください！
English: Quit surfing the net, Chief!

Japanese: す‥‥スマン。ゴメン。
English: S-Sorry... I was just, um...

Japanese: （なんか、ピリピリしてるな。 空気が‥‥）
English: (Things feel pretty tense in here...)
